messages.content:
A real estate company wants to reduce its workload and allow prospective buyers to view properties from home.

Which technology can be used to create a walkthrough that immerses the buyer into the home space?


Mixed Reality


Virtual Reality


Augmented Reality


Simulated Reality
Virtual Reality.

What is the screen door effect?


when the boundaries of the user's peripheral vision expand


when high latency causes motion sickness in the viewer


when a broader viewing range results in a more detailed image


when a low-resolution display creates a pixelated image
When a low-resolution display creates a pixelated image, leaving visible gaps between pixels that resemble a grid or a screen door, hindering the image's clarity and realism.

Why has Extended Reality (XR) recently become more common in the business world?


Individual users have much more money to spend on personal XR devices than before.


Government regulations prohibited use of XR technology until just the last few years.


The technology to create large-scale XR experiences was less affordable before now.


Employees were not interested in using XR for their work activities until recently.
The technology to create large-scale XR experiences has become more affordable, making it more accessible for businesses to implement.

What is one way a Virtual Reality work meeting could be more inclusive than a regular Microsoft Teams meeting?


It can provide an objective environment that reduces bias.


It guarantees an increase in meeting attendance.


It tracks emotions from the attendees for analytics.


It allows participants to provide immediate feedback.
Virtual Reality can provide an objective environment that reduces bias since it removes physical appearances, gender, or ethnicity, and levels the playing field for all participants.

How is a game like Pokemon Go an example of Augmented Reality?


The user must have wearable technology to play.


The digital environment is made to look very realistic.


The digital images completely replace the real world.


The user overlays digital content on the real world.

Pokemon Go is an example of Augmented Reality because the user overlays digital content, such as Pokemon characters, on the real world using the smartphone's camera and screen, adding a layer of digital content to the user's physical environment.

A customer purchases a new phone from an online store. Although the customer did not create the phone themselves, there are still carbon emissions that result from producing the phone in a factory and shipping it overseas to the customer.

Which type of emissions are described in this example?


Scope One


Scope Zero


Scope Three


Scope Two

Scope Three emissions are described in this example. Scope Three emissions are indirect emissions that result from a company's value chain activities, such as the production of their products by suppliers.

What is a benefit of a company publishing a sustainability report?


to describe its overall goals for economic growth


to describe its environmental goals and progress


to provide information on its financial stability


to publicize local environmental regulations

A benefit of a company publishing a sustainability report is to describe its environmental goals and progress. This provides transparency to stakeholders and demonstrates a company's commitment to sustainability, helping to build trust and a positive reputation.

A manufacturing company has decided to be carbon free by 2030.

What can it do to achieve this goal?


Buy carbon credits to support reforestation projects.


Switch to power generated entirely by renewable sources.


Stop manufacturing any product that contains carbon.


Capture and dispose of atmospheric carbon dioxide.

The manufacturing company can switch to power generated entirely by renewable sources, such as solar or wind power, to achieve their goal of being carbon free by 2030. They may also choose to use carbon credits to support reforestation projects or invest in technology that capture and dispose of atmospheric carbon dioxide. However, stopping the manufacturing of any product that contains carbon is not a feasible solution.

In addition to environmental issues, what does true sustainability address?


increasing data resources and security


increasing revenue and profitability


preserving social and economic resources


reducing the costs of new technologies

True sustainability addresses not only environmental issues but also social and economic issues, preserving resources that support human well-being and promoting economic growth that is equitable and benefits all stakeholders. It is a holistic approach that considers the interconnectedness of social, environmental, and economic systems.

What does it mean when a company talks about being carbon neutral?


It will stop the production of all the items that are increasing its carbon footprint.


It will capture and store atmospheric carbon dioxide generated by their factories.


It will eliminate carbon emissions by switching entirely to renewable sources.


It will balance out its carbon emissions by reducing or offsetting them.

When a company talks about being carbon neutral, it means that they will balance out their carbon emissions by reducing or offsetting them. This can be accomplished through measures such as investing in renewable energy, using carbon credits to support projects that reduce emissions, or implementing carbon offset programs such as reforestation projects. It does not necessarily mean that the company will eliminate carbon emissions by switching entirely to renewable sources or stopping the production of items that contribute to their carbon footprint.

What is one of the biggest challenges when companies are committed to sustainability?


There is a mental shift to consider sustainability in all aspects of the business.


Operations are impacted the most due to a reduction in procurement.


The surrounding community needs to understand the environmental impact of the changes.


Employees need to have guidelines about how new changes will impact their salaries.

One of the biggest challenges when companies are committed to sustainability is the mental shift required to consider sustainability in all aspects of the business. This often involves a change in mindset, culture, and priorities, which can be difficult to adapt to for some companies and employees.

How can the geographic location of data centers be beneficial for a company that is concerned about sustainability?


by distributing energy consumption across a large number of global locations


by locating data centers in countries with less restrictive regulations for emissions


by allowing the use of renewable local resources such as solar or wind energy


by placing data centers in remote areas so that their emissions are less of a concern

The geographic location of data centers can be beneficial for a company that is concerned about sustainability by allowing the use of renewable local resources, such as solar or wind energy. By strategically locating data centers in regions with high levels of renewable energy sources, companies can lower their carbon footprint and make their operations more sustainable. Additionally, leveraging renewable local resources can provide cost savings and promote the development of sustainable energy infrastructure in the surrounding community.

A large factory is monitoring machinery with digital sensors and collecting the data.

How can this data directly impact the factory's sustainability goals?


The data provides real-time information to allow the factory to quickly adjust resources.


Factory staff can use the data to schedule time more efficiently to reduce payroll expenses.


Factory employees can use data to track production trends over a specified period of time.


The data will help the factory engage with local manufacturers about supply and demand.

The data collected from monitoring machinery with digital sensors provides real-time information that allows the factory to quickly adjust resources to reduce waste and energy consumption. By analyzing the data, the factory can identify inefficiencies and areas for improvement, leading to better resource utilization and reduced environmental impact, directly impacting the factory's sustainability goals.

How can myNav Green Cloud Advisor help an Accenture client achieve a clear emissions profile for their data centers?


by using special software to e-design data centers with renewable energy sources


by applying a single green solution plan to all clients regardless of location or readiness


by establishing the client's existing data center energy consumption for comparison


by allowing the client to retrieve and store the largest quantities of data possible

myNav Green Cloud Advisor can help an Accenture client achieve a clear emissions profile for their data centers by establishing the client's existing data center energy consumption as a baseline for comparison. Using tools such as energy modeling, data analytics, and benchmarking, myNav Green Cloud Advisor calculates the carbon footprint of a client's data center operation and identifies opportunities for improvement. With this information, the client can develop targeted strategies to reduce emissions, optimize energy usage and costs, and improve their overall sustainability performance.

What is an important conclusion of climate prediction models?


Temperature changes under two degrees Celsius are insignificant.


Increase in global temperature will continue, but can be reduced.


Accuracy in climate change reporting by businesses is impossible.


Reporting on climate change is often avoided by businesses.

An important conclusion of climate prediction models is that the increase in global temperature will continue, but can be reduced if significant steps are taken to mitigate carbon emissions and shift to a low-carbon economy. This implies that the actions taken today will have a significant impact on the future state of the climate. Reporting on climate change by businesses is increasingly relevant, and reporting accuracy is improving due to various international frameworks providing guidance on how to report, driven by increasing pressure from stakeholders and society at large. Businesses are expected to play a key role in reducing carbon emissions and transitioning towards a more sustainable future.

Where do most of a software-focused company's carbon emissions typically come from?


public relations and marketing


corporate operations


finance and accounting


product manufacturing

For a software-focused company, most of the carbon emissions typically come from corporate operations, such as powering data centers, cooling equipment, running office buildings, and employee travel. These activities are energy-intensive and often rely on fossil fuels, resulting in significant carbon emissions. Manufacturers' emissions are typically lower in comparison to other industries, since the majority of the software-focused companies are not directly involved in the physical product manufacturing process.

Accenture is one of several hundred companies that has signed on to the United Nations Global Compact (UNGC) Business Ambition pledge.

What is the goal of this pledge?


to donate to charity in exchange for exemption from regulations


to switch entirely to solar power at all office locations worldwide


to keep global warming below a 1.5 Celsius increase from pre-industrial levels


to reverse all effects of global warming within the next decade

The goal of the UNGC Business Ambition pledge is to keep global warming below a 1.5 Celsius increase from pre-industrial levels. Signatories of this pledge commit to setting science-based emissions reduction targets in line with the goals of the Paris Agreement on climate change and to accelerate their actions to achieve these targets. The pledge aims to encourage companies to take urgent and ambitious action to address the climate crisis and contribute to a more sustainable future.

What is an example of new technology having a NEGATIVE impact on sustainability?


using the cloud to analyze environmental data on a global scale


using multiple servers to maintain a cryptocurrency operation


using automation to shut down unused electric devices


using smart meters to track energy consumption in homes

An example of new technology having a negative impact on sustainability is using multiple servers to maintain a cryptocurrency operation. Cryptocurrency mining is energy-intensive and requires a significant amount of computing power to operate effectively, resulting in high carbon emissions. Multiple servers used for mining purposes contribute to increased energy consumption and carbon emissions from the production of electricity generated from fossil fuels.

How can edge computing be used to improve sustainability?


by using sensors to track real-time data about energy usage


by instantly converting older machines to use renewable energy


by allowing devices to operate without a network connection


by requiring all devices to connect directly to government servers

Edge computing can be used to improve sustainability by using sensors to track real-time data about energy usage. With edge computing, data can be processed at the edge of the network, closer to the source of the data, reducing the need to transmit data back and forth to a centralized data center. This enables the use of sensors and IoT devices to collect and analyze real-time data on energy usage, allowing for more efficient and sustainable energy management. By identifying areas of waste and optimizing energy usage in real-time, edge computing can help reduce energy consumption, lower emissions, and improve sustainability.
